shape.lat: number — user latitude in decimal degrees
shape.lon: number — user longitude in decimal degrees
shape.label: string — human-readable location label (e.g. detected address or the text the user typed)
shape.source: string — one of 'auto' | 'manual', how the location was obtained

notes: section-1 (Location acquisition) produces exactly one resolved location object once location is established by either the automatic Geolocation path or the manual-entry path. section-2 (store lookup) consumes only lat and lon to build the Overpass around-filter query; label and source are passed through for display by downstream sections. section-2 must not call any geolocation or geocoding logic itself.
